name: Measure Angular Width of Eagle Nebula Pillar Using Line Profile
description:
The Eagle Nebula H-alpha image contains the famous 'Pillars of Creation'. Your task is to measure the angular width of the tallest pillar at a specific cross-section using AstroImageJ and the FITS image located at ~/AstroImages/eagle_pillar/656nmos.fits. Determine the target Y-coordinate from ~/AstroImages/eagle_pillar/measurement_guide.txt and the image plate scale from ~/AstroImages/eagle_pillar/plate_scale_info.txt. Measure the Full Width at Half Maximum (FWHM) in pixels of the pillar feature at the specified coordinate and calculate the angular width in arcseconds. Create a results file at ~/AstroImages/eagle_pillar/pillar_width_results.txt containing exactly these four lines with your numeric values:

width_pixels: <value>
plate_scale: <value>
width_arcsec: <value>
y_coordinate: <value>
